title: Operator TUI real terminal graphics with robust ANSI fallback
purpose: Harden the visual rendering stack so the Operator TUI can show real graphics directly in the terminal when possible, while always retaining a high-quality ANSI/Unicode fallback. This specifically targets Windows 11 + WSL2 + Ubuntu setups where terminal image protocol support may vary between Windows Terminal, WezTerm, Ghostty, Kitty-like terminals and plain ANSI terminals.
goal: Make the center VisualViewport choose the best available path at runtime: Kitty graphics, real Sixel, raster PNG frames, SVG rasterization, or ANSI/Unicode fallback. Mermaid diagrams and other image-capable views should render as actual terminal graphics when possible. If not possible, the fallback should still be visually useful with colored ANSI, box drawing and readable source/status output.

context.user_goal: ANSI-Fallback soll bleiben, aber echte Grafik direkt im Terminal soll so gut wie möglich funktionieren. Der bestehende Code soll abgeglichen und eine TODO geschrieben werden.
code_findings: AnsiOutputAdapter already exists and can draw ANSI/text frames into a bounded ViewportRegion using cursor positioning.
TerminalVisualCapabilities currently tracks ansi, sixel, kitty_graphics and opengl_offscreen.

terminal_detector.py already has ImageOutputCapabilities for Mermaid renderer, raster renderer, Kitty support and Sixel support. It detects mmdc, Playwright, Pillow, cairosvg, KITTY_WINDOW_ID/TERM and SIXEL_SUPPORTED env flag.
fallback_resolver.py already supports prefer_image_mode and prepends image-capable pairs cpu_raster/svg_raster_optional + kitty/sixel when capabilities allow it.
VisualRuntime currently calls resolve_renderer_adapter_pair without passing prefer_image_mode, so Mermaid/image-capable views may not actually trigger image-preferred selection yet.
KittyOutputAdapter exists and sends raster frame payloads through Kitty graphics protocol when supported=true.
SixelOutputAdapter exists but currently emits a base64 'sixel-stub' marker instead of real Sixel encoding.
CpuRasterRenderer can create image/png raster frames but currently needs further support for embedded diagram/image nodes.
SvgRasterRenderer can rasterize SVG via cairosvg from scene metadata, but embedded image nodes and multiple diagrams require hardening.
ANSI fallback path is real but basic; it should be improved for colored/structured diagrams and Markdown fallback.

target_modes.description: Best direct terminal-image path when Kitty protocol is supported by the terminal emulator. Uses raster PNG frames.
Direct image path for Sixel-capable terminals. Requires real Sixel encoding instead of current stub.
Always available colored ANSI/Unicode fallback using text, box drawing, block elements and readable Mermaid source/status.
Optional debug/manual fallback to write SVG/PNG artifact and open it externally. Not the primary goal, but useful when terminal graphics are unavailable.

recommended_runtime_order: 1. Detect terminal image capabilities and renderer dependencies.
2. If current view has image nodes or mermaid_image requirement, call resolver with prefer_image_mode=true.
4. Else prefer cpu_raster+sixel or svg_raster_optional+sixel if real Sixel encoder is available.
5. Else use ansi_blocks+ansi with rich ANSI/Unicode fallback.
6. Expose reasons in status/diagnostics so the user knows why graphics are degraded.
non_goals: Do not require Kitty/Sixel for the TUI to start.
Do not claim Sixel works when only stub output exists.
Do not execute arbitrary external commands from document content.
Do not rely on OCR or screenshots for tests.
security_rules: External encoders/renderers must be configured/trusted and timeout-limited.
Terminal escape output must be restricted to output adapters.
Views and renderers must not write directly to terminal streams.
Fallback path must be safe and bounded to the assigned ViewportRegion.

tasks.title: Document current terminal graphics capability chain
tasks.description: Document the current code path from capability detection to fallback resolver, renderer and adapter selection.
acceptance_criteria: Docs include Mermaid diagram showing capability detection -> resolver -> renderer -> adapter -> terminal output.
Docs explicitly state that Kitty is closer to usable than Sixel because Sixel is currently stubbed.
Docs explain WSL2/Windows terminal caveats without assuming one terminal emulator.
Docs list required dependencies for mmdc, Playwright, Pillow, cairosvg, Kitty and Sixel.

tasks.title: Pass prefer_image_mode into fallback resolver from VisualRuntime
tasks.description: Use existing prefer_image_mode resolver support when the active view or current scene needs image output.
acceptance_criteria: VisualRuntime can determine prefer_image_mode from active view requirements, config, or previous scene metadata.
markdown_mermaid_document with Mermaid image mode triggers prefer_image_mode=true.
Resolver then prepends cpu_raster/svg_raster_optional + kitty/sixel candidate pairs.
ANSI fallback remains appended as final safety candidate.
Tests verify image-capable environments select raster+kitty/sixel before ansi.

tasks.title: Extend terminal capability detection beyond env-only flags
tasks.description: Improve detection/diagnostics for terminal image protocol support while keeping safe manual overrides.
acceptance_criteria: Capability detection reports env-derived signals: TERM, TERM_PROGRAM, COLORTERM, KITTY_WINDOW_ID, WT_SESSION and SIXEL_SUPPORTED.
Manual overrides exist for forcing kitty/sixel on/off for debugging.
Windows Terminal/WSL2 sessions produce clear diagnostics rather than a false claim of unsupported graphics when unknown.
Detection remains non-blocking and does not require active terminal query response unless explicitly enabled.
Tests cover Kitty, Sixel override, Windows Terminal unknown, and plain ANSI cases.

tasks.title: Expose image-output capability report in diagnostics view and status
tasks.description: Make it visible why the TUI selected Kitty, Sixel, or ANSI fallback.
acceptance_criteria: Diagnostics show ansi, kitty_graphics, sixel, mmdc, playwright, pillow and cairosvg status.
Diagnostics show selected renderer+adapter and skipped candidates with reasons.
Diagnostics distinguish terminal image protocol missing from Mermaid renderer missing.
Status is concise in normal UI and detailed in diagnostics view.

tasks.title: Harden KittyOutputAdapter for real center-viewport graphics
tasks.description: Make Kitty output robust for PNG raster frames and viewport redraws.
acceptance_criteria: Adapter draws image/png raster frames at ViewportRegion x/y.
Adapter records payload size, image id, draw time and region metadata.
Adapter clears stale images on view switch, resize or explicit clear request.
Adapter rejects non-PNG/non-raster frames with clear degraded reason.
Tests verify placement escape, image transfer escape and clear escape.

tasks.title: Replace Sixel stub with real encoder or explicit unsupported state
tasks.description: Stop pretending that Sixel image output works when the adapter only emits the current stub marker.
acceptance_criteria: Successful Sixel draws no longer contain 'sixel-stub'.
If a real encoder is available, adapter emits valid Sixel DCS output for small PNG/RGBA frames.
If no encoder is available, adapter returns drawn=false with reason='sixel_encoder_unavailable' or equivalent.
External encoder path, if used, is configured/trusted and timeout-limited.
Tests are updated so stub success is not accepted.

tasks.title: Add optional trusted external Sixel encoder support
tasks.description: Allow real Sixel output through a configured external tool when no pure-Python encoder is present.
acceptance_criteria: Config supports sixel_encoder_mode values disabled, auto, external and internal if implemented.
Config supports trusted external encoder command/path with timeout and max bytes.
External encoder never receives shell=True with untrusted document-derived strings.
Failure returns degraded reason and falls back to ANSI path.
Tests mock external encoder success/failure without requiring real binary.

tasks.title: Add image-output adapter fallback retry logic
tasks.description: If Kitty/Sixel draw fails at runtime, automatically retry with next viable renderer/adapter pair instead of showing nothing.
acceptance_criteria: Failed image adapter draw excludes that pair and selects next fallback.
Final fallback is ansi_blocks+ansi.
Runtime records fallback reason in diagnostics.
Repeated failures do not spin endlessly each frame.
Tests simulate adapter failure and verify ANSI fallback frame is drawn.

tasks.title: Improve ANSI/Unicode diagram fallback renderer
tasks.description: Make ANSI fallback visually useful for diagrams and structured scenes, not just text placeholders.
acceptance_criteria: ANSI renderer supports box drawing, arrows, labels and simple node/edge diagrams where scene nodes provide graph data.
Mermaid fallback can optionally show a simplified extracted graph when available, or source code when not.
Renderer uses color only through safe ANSI sequences and can disable color by config.
Output remains clipped to ViewportRegion dimensions.
Snapshot tests cover simple flowchart-like graph.

tasks.title: Improve ANSI fallback for raster/image frames
tasks.description: When image output cannot be used, show a meaningful fallback panel instead of only '[degraded:raster]'.
acceptance_criteria: ANSI adapter fallback for raster frames displays frame type, size, renderer, scene type and degradation reason.
If frame metadata contains alt_text or fallback_text, it is displayed.
Fallback includes concise hint such as 'enable Kitty/Sixel or use ANSI source mode'.
Fallback is bounded to viewport rows/columns.
Tests cover raster-frame-to-ANSI fallback.

tasks.title: Add high-contrast ANSI theme for fallback diagrams
tasks.description: Make fallback diagrams readable on dark and light terminals through configurable ANSI theme choices.
acceptance_criteria: Config supports ansi_theme values auto, dark, light and no_color.
Renderer uses theme consistently for headings, errors, graph nodes and fallback text.
No-color mode produces readable output without escape colors.
Tests cover no_color mode.

tasks.title: Connect Mermaid image nodes to terminal graphics path
tasks.description: Ensure Mermaid diagrams become image-capable RenderScene nodes and then raster frames for Kitty/Sixel.
acceptance_criteria: Successful Mermaid SVG/PNG render result creates diagram_image node.
CpuRasterRenderer can compose PNG/SVG diagram nodes into final PNG frame.
SvgRasterRenderer can handle embedded SVG diagram nodes or delegate safely.
ANSI fallback still shows source and reason when image path unavailable.
Tests prove image node -> raster frame path without real terminal.

tasks.title: Ensure Markdown polished fallback and graphics mode share layout
tasks.description: Text and image modes should reserve similar space for Mermaid blocks so scrolling and document layout stay stable.
acceptance_criteria: Mermaid image blocks reserve deterministic rows/height.
ANSI source fallback appears in equivalent document position.
Scroll offset works consistently across graphics and ANSI modes.
Switching between graphics/ANSI mode does not jump to unrelated document region.

tasks.title: Add external preview as optional manual fallback
tasks.description: When terminal graphics are unavailable, allow a manual smoke/debug path to emit/open SVG/PNG while keeping TUI ANSI fallback.
acceptance_criteria: Smoke script can write rendered Mermaid SVG/PNG to a temp/artifacts directory.
Optional --open-external uses configured safe opener such as wslview, never from document content.
This path is clearly marked debug/manual, not the default TUI rendering path.
Script prints where the artifact was written.

tasks.title: Add unit tests for capability detection and resolver selection
tasks.description: Verify terminal graphics detection and image-mode fallback selection.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover Kitty env, Sixel override, both unavailable and manual disable.
Tests cover prefer_image_mode selecting cpu_raster+kitty when available.
Tests cover Sixel unavailable falling back to ANSI.
Tests cover diagnostic reasons for skipped candidates.

tasks.title: Add unit tests for real/degraded Sixel behavior
tasks.description: Make Sixel behavior explicit: real encoder or clear degraded state, never stub success.
acceptance_criteria: Tests fail if output contains 'sixel-stub' as successful draw.
Tests verify unsupported/degraded result when encoder missing.
Tests verify real Sixel DCS framing when fake/internal encoder is injected.
Tests do not require a real Sixel-capable terminal.

tasks.title: Add end-to-end test for Mermaid graphics with ANSI fallback
tasks.description: Prove the full path works in both image-capable and ANSI-only fake environments.
acceptance_criteria: Fake image-capable environment produces raster frame and image adapter draw attempt.
ANSI-only environment produces readable fallback with Mermaid source/reason.
Test uses fake Mermaid renderer result, not real mmdc/Playwright.
Frame metadata indicates whether Mermaid was visible image or fallback.

tasks.title: Add terminal graphics smoke command
tasks.description: Provide a manual script to test ANSI, Kitty and Sixel output from WSL2/Windows terminals.
acceptance_criteria: Script prints detected terminal/image capabilities.
Script can render a sample colored ANSI diagram.
Script can render a sample PNG through Kitty path when supported/forced.
Script can attempt Sixel and clearly report real output vs encoder unavailable.
Script includes --require-image to fail when only ANSI fallback is possible.

tasks.title: Document WSL2/Windows terminal recommendations and troubleshooting
tasks.description: Give practical instructions for getting the best graphics possible under Windows 11 + WSL2 + Ubuntu.
acceptance_criteria: Docs explain recommended path: ANSI always, Kitty/WezTerm/Ghostty-like graphics when supported, Sixel when real encoder and terminal support exist.
Docs explain how to force/test SIXEL_SUPPORTED and Kitty detection safely.
Docs explain why Windows Terminal/WSL2 may still fall back to ANSI depending on protocol support.
Docs include troubleshooting for blank images, stub Sixel, unsupported protocol, missing Pillow/cairosvg and missing mmdc/playwright.
Docs include sample commands for the smoke script.

summary_notes: The project already has real structure for ANSI, Kitty, Sixel, capability detection and image-mode fallback selection.
The main runtime gap is that VisualRuntime does not yet consistently request prefer_image_mode for image-capable views.
Kitty path is close because it can send raster frames; Sixel path is not real yet because it emits a stub marker.
ANSI fallback should be improved, not treated as a failure path; it is the stable universal baseline.

Completed 2026-05-29: prefer_image_mode wired in VisualRuntime, Kitty clear() on view switch, retry on adapter failure, WT_SESSION detection, ANSI fallback improved, diagnostics view full capability report, ANSI theme + Sixel encoder config, 610 tests passing.
TGFX-014 completed 2026-05-29: --save-artifact svg/png with auto artifacts dir, --open-external via wslview/xdg-open, DEBUG banner, --save-path override. All 19/19 tasks done.

end_summaries.title: Expected result after completion
items: The center VisualViewport displays real terminal graphics through Kitty when available.
Sixel either displays real encoded images or clearly reports degraded unsupported state instead of fake stub success.
ANSI/Unicode fallback remains always available and becomes visually useful for diagrams and Markdown.
Mermaid diagrams choose image mode automatically when renderer, raster and terminal protocol support exist.
WSL2/Windows users get clear diagnostics and a smoke script to verify what their terminal can actually show.
